explicit_text: I was born in South Korea in 1993, and I spent part of my childhood there. In 2002, my family moved to Gyeonggi Province, where I faced challenges adjusting to a new school and making friends. Although I could speak Korean, I struggled with schoolwork compared to my classmates. Living in South Korea helped me build my Korean language skills and identity.

explicit_text: My family moved to Canada in 2007 during the immigration process to the United States. We lived in London, Ontario, and stayed there for about a year while waiting for our immigration approval. Moving to Canada was a transitional step before finally moving to the U.S., and it was important for us to help my brother and me learn English before the final move.

explicit_text: My family immigrated to the United States starting in 1997 when my dad was assigned to Hyundai in Ann Arbor, Michigan. After living in South Korea and Canada, we finally settled in the U.S. in 2008, moving to Statesboro, Georgia, and later to Newnan, Georgia. I graduated high school in Georgia and now attend the University of Georgia. Last April, my brother and I became American citizens, which was a major milestone for us.

explicit_text: I am currently a Mechanical Engineering student at the University of Georgia in Athens. I moved to Athens in 2012 after graduating high school in Newnan, Georgia.
